property.name: java.library.path
property.value:

oConfigScripts;.

testcase.name: numericGradeTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Course( ) Reading the value of private boolean numericGrade ~> false ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the return value of the getter to the value of the attribute (ignoring the visibility modifier): ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private boolean numericGrade ~> false Checking whether isNumericGrade( ) returns false ~> false (true) ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the value of the attribute before and after setting a new value using the setter (ignoring the attribute's visibility modifier) ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private boolean numericGrade ~> false Calling setNumericGrade( true ) Checking whether private boolean numericGrade is true ~> true (true) ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the value of the attribute before and after setting a new value using the setter (ignoring the attribute's visibility modifier) ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private boolean numericGrade ~> true Calling setNumericGrade( false ) Checking whether private boolean numericGrade is false ~> false (true) ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the return value of the getter to the value of the attribute after setting a new value directly (ignoring the visibility modifier): ----------------------------------------------------- Assigning value true to private boolean numericGrade Checking whether isNumericGrade( ) returns true ~> true (true) ✓

testcase.name: courseCodeTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Course( ) Calling setCourseCode( 968066, 'A' ) Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "968066A" ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 495997, 'a' ) Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "495997A" ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 714087, 'p' ) Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "714087P" ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 218624, 's' ) Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "218624S" ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the return value of the getter to the value of the attribute after setting a new value directly (ignoring the visibility modifier): ----------------------------------------------------- Assigning value "837845S" to private String courseCode Checking whether getCourseCode( ) returns a String matching "837845S" ~> "837845S" (true) ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 1, 'S' ) Calling getCourseCode( ) ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 0, 'S' ) Calling getCourseCode( ) Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "1S" ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 999999, 'S' ) Calling getCourseCode( ) Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "999999S" ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 1000000, 'S' ) Calling getCourseCode( ) Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "999999S" ✓ Calling setCourseCode( -764074, 'P' ) Calling getCourseCode( ) Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "999999S" ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 393552, 'K' ) Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "999999S" Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "999999S" ✓ Calling getCourseCode( ) Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "999999S" ✓

testcase.name: periodTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Course( ) Reading the value of private int period ~> 0 ✓ Calling setPeriod( 1 ) Checking whether private int period is 1 ~> 1 (true) Calling setPeriod( 5 ) Checking whether private int period is 5 ~> 5 (true) Calling setPeriod( 0 ) Checking whether private int period is within [1, 5] ~> 5 (true) Calling setPeriod( 6 ) Checking whether private int period is within [1, 5] ~> 5 (true) Reading the value of private int period ~> 5 ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the value of the attribute before and after setting a new value using the setter (ignoring the attribute's visibility modifier) ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private int period ~> 5 Calling setPeriod( 1 ) Checking whether private int period is 1 ~> 1 (true) ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the return value of the getter to the value of the attribute after setting a new value directly (ignoring the visibility modifier): ----------------------------------------------------- Assigning value 2 to private int period Checking whether getPeriod( ) returns 2 ~> 2 (true) ✓ Calling setPeriod( -59 ) Calling getPeriod( ) ✓ Calling setPeriod( 85 ) Checking whether getPeriod( ) returns 2 ~> 2 (true) ✓

testcase.name: creditsTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Course( ) Reading the value of private double credits ~> 0.0 ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the value of the attribute before and after setting a new value using the setter (ignoring the attribute's visibility modifier) ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private double credits ~> 0.0 Calling setCredits( 55.0 ) Checking whether private double credits is 55.0 ~> 55.0 (true) ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the value of the attribute before and after setting a new value using the setter (ignoring the attribute's visibility modifier) ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private double credits ~> 55.0 Calling setCredits( 0.0 ) Checking whether private double credits is 0.0 ~> 0.0 (true) ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the value of the attribute before and after setting a new value using the setter (ignoring the attribute's visibility modifier) ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private double credits ~> 0.0 Calling setCredits( 37.0 ) Checking whether private double credits is 37.0 ~> 37.0 (true) ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the return value of the getter to the value of the attribute after setting a new value directly (ignoring the visibility modifier): ----------------------------------------------------- Assigning value 8.0 to private double credits Checking whether getCredits( ) returns 8.0 ~> 8.0 (true) ✓ Calling setCredits( -57.0 ) Calling getCredits( ) ✓ Calling setCredits( 92.0 ) Calling getCredits( ) ✓

testcase.name: constructorTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Course( ) Calling new Course( "qwijtbacff", 145936, 'P', 0, 1, 6.0, true ) Calling new Course( [145936P ( 6.00 cr), "qwijtbacff". Optional, period: 1.] ) Checking whether the two course objects are equal: => Course 2: Reading the value of private String name ~> "qwijtbacff" => Course 1: Checking whether private String name is "qwijtbacff" ~> "qwijtbacff" (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private String courseCode ~> "145936P" => Course 1: Checking whether the value of private String courseCode matches "145936P" ~> "145936P" (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private Character courseBase ~> 'P' => Course 1: Checking whether private Character courseBase is 'P' ~> 'P' (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private int period ~> 1 => Course 1: Checking whether private int period is 1 ~> 1 (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private double credits ~> 6.0 => Course 1: Checking whether private double credits is 6.0 ~> 6.0 (true) ✓ => Course 2: Reading the value of private boolean numericGrade ~> true => Course 1: Checking whether private boolean numericGrade is true ~> true (true) ✓ => The course objects are equal.

testcase.name: courseTypeTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Course( ) Calling setCourseType( 0 ) Reading the value of private int courseType ~> 0 ✓ Calling getCourseType( ) ✓ Checking whether getCourseTypeString( ) returns a String matching "(?i)Optional" ~> "Optional" (true) Reading the value of private int courseType ~> 0 ✓ Calling setCourseType( 1 ) Reading the value of private int courseType ~> 1 ✓ Calling getCourseType( ) ✓ Checking whether getCourseTypeString( ) returns a String matching "(?i)Mandatory" ~> "Mandatory" (true) Reading the value of private int courseType ~> 1 ✓ Calling setCourseType( -53 ) Calling getCourseType( ) ✓ Reading the value of private int courseType ~> 1 ✓ Checking whether getCourseTypeString( ) returns a String matching "(?i)Mandatory" ~> "Mandatory" (true) Reading the value of private int courseType ~> 1 ✓ Calling setCourseType( 0 ) Calling setCourseType( 28 ) Calling getCourseType( ) ✓ Reading the value of private int courseType ~> 0 ✓ Checking whether getCourseTypeString( ) returns a String matching "(?i)Optional" ~> "Optional" (true) Reading the value of private int courseType ~> 0 ✓

testcase.name: constructorParameterTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Course( ) Calling new Course( null, -100000, 'A', -1, 6, -1.0, true ) Reading the value of private int courseType ~> 0 ✓ Reading the value of private int period ~> 0 ✓ Reading the value of private double credits ~> 0.0 ✓

testcase.name: nameTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Course( ) Calling setName( "szeccnvbri" ) ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the value of the attribute before and after setting a new value using the setter (ignoring the attribute's visibility modifier) ----------------------------------------------------- Reading the value of private String name ~> "szeccnvbri" The current value and the new value are the same. The setter should update the current value. ~> The test will be inconclusive... Calling setName( "szeccnvbri" ) Checking whether private String name is "szeccnvbri" ~> "szeccnvbri" (true) ✓ ----------------------------------------------------- Comparing the return value of the getter to the value of the attribute after setting a new value directly (ignoring the visibility modifier): ----------------------------------------------------- Assigning value "szeccnvbri" to private String name Checking whether getName( ) returns "szeccnvbri" ~> "szeccnvbri" (true) ✓ Calling setName( null ) Reading the value of private String name ~> "szeccnvbri" ✓ Calling setName( "" ) Reading the value of private String name ~> "szeccnvbri" ✓

testcase.name: courseBaseTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Course( ) Calling setCourseCode( 248147, 'A' ) Reading the value of private Character courseBase ~> 'A' ✓ Calling getCourseBase( ) ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 990448, 'a' ) Calling getCourseBase( ) ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 927774, 'p' ) Calling getCourseBase( ) ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 566577, 's' ) Calling getCourseBase( ) ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 1590029, 'A' ) Calling getCourseBase( ) ✓ Calling setCourseCode( 343257, 'K' ) Reading the value of private Character courseBase ~> 'S' ✓ Calling getCourseBase( ) ✓

testcase.name: toStringTests
testcase.system-out: Calling new Course( ) Calling new Course( "axxnvfiioh", 59353, 'P', 0, 3, 41.0, true ) Calling toString( ) The output should match the regular expression pattern: "\[59353P \( 41\.?00 cr\), "axxnvfiioh"\.? Optional, period: 3\.?\]" Attempting to locate the starting point of the regular expression pattern: "\[59353" ✓ ✓ Calling new Course( "fhpndomrsx", 635830, 'P', 0, 3, 43.0, false ) Calling toString( ) The output should match the regular expression pattern: "\[635830P \( 43\.?00 cr\), "fhpndomrsx"\.? Optional, period: 3\.?\]" Attempting to locate the starting point of the regular expression pattern: "\[63583" ✓ ✓ Calling new Course( "zxcadmrocn", 249859, 'A', 1, 2, 9.0, false ) Calling toString( ) The output should match the regular expression pattern: "\[249859A \( 9\.?00 cr\), "zxcadmrocn"\.? Mandatory, period: 2\.?\]" Attempting to locate the starting point of the regular expression pattern: "\[24985" ✓ ✓
